Video.summary: In this episode Andy and his friend Scotty take on Kodiak Island, Alaska for an adventure of Salmon fishing and hunting black tail deer. Whilst traversing steep mountains in the company of Kodiak brown bears and taking on cold Alaskan waters, Andy presents useful information for fellow outdoor enthusiasts all whilst building friendships with the great people of Kodiak Big Timber Lodge.
Video.title: Wildbound - "Alaska"

Video.summary: In this episode Andy and his son, Logan visit the beautiful country of New Zealand. From visiting Vineyards and sampling wine, to helicopter hunts for Himalayan Tahr, this episode is everything that is a hunters dream. As well as harvesting the wild game New Zealand has to offer Andy also learns about the importance of the conservation efforts that hunting has in this country from locals in which he spends time with.
Video.title: Wildbound - "New Zealand"

Video.summary: Join Rush and his good friend Anthony Solmo for an epic day of fishing in the stunning waters of Key West, Florida! Leaving the Odyssea at the dock, they hop on a bay boat outfitted with a trolling motor for the ultimate inshore adventure. Gliding quietly through the shallows, they target a variety of species—including hard-pulling Tarpon and explosive topwater Jacks—leaving Rush thinking, “A guy could get used to this kind of fishing!”
Video.title: "Bay Day"

Video.summary: For the first time in history, the World English Sporting Clays Championship was held in the United States at the incredible M&M Hunting Preserve owned by the Matarese family in New Jersey. Watch as Winchester’s best shooters vie for Gold as they compete against over 2,500 shooters and 17 countries. We’ll also join the Kids and Clays foundation in Florida as they host an event that will most certainly touch your soul.
Video.title: "Clays With Purpose"

Video.summary: Rooted in history, Apalachicola, Florida, is where Capt. Jonathan Moss finds himself today, scouting the marsh for hungry backcountry dwellers.
Video.title: "Operation Apalach"

Video.summary: In this episode Andy goes back to his home farm in Alabama for deer and quail hunting. Not only does he make incredible dishes with his former ‘masterchef’ contestant Alex which he serves to local children in need but he explains how hunting is never about “just the hunt” but about the camaraderie, friendship and memories made through the pursuit of the harvest.
Video.title: Wildbound - "Alabama"

Video.summary: A day of reef fishing in Islamorada turns into a Kingfish and Yellowtail frenzy for Captains Scott and Steve.
Video.title: "Silver And Gold"

Video.summary: It only takes 3 fish to fill the Yeti cooler when Capt. Scott fishes the top with kites for Wahoo and Steve pulls a big Mutton Snapper off the bottom.
Video.title: "Wahoos On Kites"

Video.summary: Captains Scott and Steve take a long run into the Gulf from Stock Island and load the coolers with Cobia and huge Yellowtail Snapper.
Video.title: "Stock Island Grocery Run"

Video.summary: Captains Scott and Steve head west from Key West to battle with big kingfish and end up with plenty of meat for the smoker.
Video.title: "Kingfishing West Of Key West"

Video.summary: Captains Scott and Steve head out early out of Key West, Scott picks away at the Yellowtail Snapper while Steve plays around with Cero Mackerel on fly.
Video.title: "Key West Yellowtails And Cero On Fly"

Image.alt: "Key West Yellowtails And Cero On Fly"
"Key West Yellowtails And Cero On Fly"
"Key West Yellowtails And Cero On Fly"

Video.summary: Captains Scott and Steve catch Bonitas for bait and slow troll them around wrecks for Wahoo and Kingfish.
Video.title: "Trolling Bonitas Out Of Key West"

Video.summary: Capt. Jared travels to Venice, Louisiana, where the Mississippi River meets the Gulf, joining Capt. Garrett George and Capt. Jesse Leboeuf for an action-packed offshore adventure in pursuit of Redfish and Tuna.
Video.title: "LA Tuna Pt 1"

Video.summary: In this episode, Slade and his son take on the turkey woods for an afternoon hunt. Although the woods remained quiet, a tom soon snuck out of the woods and on the end of Bentley's barrel! After that Slade was able to help a client of his harvest his first turkey! And boy was it an action packed hunt! Jakes, hens, and tons of gobbles, This hunt is spectacular! Lastly, Slade introduces a property that we have already made a video on, but with just how amazing the land is, we had to include it! Floweree is the epitome of a sportsman's paradise!
Video.title: "Two More Turkeys Down in Mississippi!!!"

Video.summary: On this episode of Hunting Land Man, Slade, Ryan and Bearcub take on the midwest! After some tough hunting, the group is able to make a move to get in position on a BIG midwestern gobbler! Bearcub was able to harvest him after working the bird for almost 45 minutes! Slade and Ryan also do some Pre-Deer season work, and establish where the fall food-plots will be!
Video.title: "Missouri Turkey Tour!!"

Video.summary: SoCal summer is calling, and the Local Knowledge crew is back on Ali’s home turf in San Diego, California. With reports of hot bites and fish moving in, the crew faces a classic Southern California dilemma — chase big yellowtail around the Coronado Islands or head north in search of bluefin tuna. The options are plenty, the challenges are real, and that’s exactly what keeps fishing in SoCal so exciting.
Video.title: "Dog Gone Yellow"

Video.summary: Heavy hearts find comfort in cherished memories as the Local Knowledge crew mourns the loss of one of the greatest members of the fishing industry. Looking back on several of our episodes with our dear friend Dave Salazar, we celebrate the life of this amazing fishermen, family man, and visionary behind the world renowned Casa Vieja Lodge.
Video.title: "El Jefe"

Video.summary: The ability to overcome obstacles and challenging conditions can be key to success, but when fishing in Key West, cold temps are rarely the hurdle. For Rush and Ali, it's another day on the water where patience and a plan pays off with Cobia and Amberjack action that gets the rods bent after a cold weather spell in the Keys.
Video.title: "Cobia So Cold"

Video.summary: When it comes to fishing in Key West, you can often get a lot done in a little bit of time. After an early check in to Ocean’s Edge Resort and a mid day departure from then dock, Ali looks to Rush to put him on an afternoon bite of tuna and bottom fish that few other places can offer. If Rush is going to deliver, he will need to make a plan and stick to it to make the best use of this limited time.
Video.title: "Afternoon Delight"

Video.summary: For Ali and Rush, one of the best aspects of fishing is being able to share experiences on the water with interesting people. On this episode we invite luxury watch enthusiast Eric Rivera - AKA "Watch Eric" to the Florida Keys to target Permit and Amberjack in the Gulf.
Video.title: "Watch Eric"

Video.summary: Despite adverse conditions, Capt. Jonathan Moss and long time friend, Capt. Adam Whitson, are heading out on the water in search of the Crystal River Inshore Slam.
Video.title: "Weather or Not"

Video.summary: Nestled in the hills of Ohio just west of the Appalachians is a very special deer camp ripe with memories from deer season’s past. With the development of the 350 Legend straight-walled cartridge by Winchester in 2019, a world of opportunities now existed in states like Ohio and Michigan. In this episode, with the 350 Legend in hand, three mature whitetails meet their maker, from the same blind, over the course of three seasons.
Video.title: "Remembering A Legend"

Video.summary: Captain Scott Walker heads swordfishing out of Hawks Cay with his friend, retired WWE wrestler Travis Tomko.
Video.title: "Swordfishing with Travis Tomko"

Video.summary: In this episode of Flats Class, CA travels to Venice, Louisiana to chase monster redfish on the flats with local expert Capt. Eric Newman.
Video.title: "Jumbo-laya"

Video.summary: Mark is with guest and great friend Jimmy Jennings. Jimmy helped get The Direction, Marks dream of filming outdoor TV, to reality. His team at One Call Concepts supported Marks idea of delivering The Call 811, the national call before you dig number and safe digging message, to the outdoor viewer thru outdoor TV a reality. It was Mark’s dream as a kit to host an outdoor show. - Jimmy and his team took a risk that the show would take off and it did. Now airing on multiple outdoor networks and digital distribution platforms, Jimmy’s belief and Mark’s crazy dream has taken the safe digging message to tens of millions of viewers over the last 9 years. Listen to these two reminisce on past fishing trips and watch as they go to one of Mark’s favorite fishing lakes in KY for some crazy fall largemouth bass fishing action!
Video.title: "Bassin’ and Reminiscing"

Video.summary: On this episode, we travel to Missouri River near Chamberlain, SD and connect with the South Dakota chapter of Paralyzed Veterans of America for the Joel Niemeyer "Gone Fishing" event. We get to guide and share the story of 100 year old WW2 Veteran Kenneth Carlson and his family. We also honor our next Rochester, MN Scheels Hometown Hero, Dustin Draeger!
Video.title: "Paralyzed Veterans Of America"

Video.summary: On this episode, we travel north of the border for an unforgettable PART 1 Canadian fly-in fishing trip with Mandatory Fun Outdoors. We also highlight our next Scheels Hometown Hero, Jay Strande!
Video.title: "Canadian Fly-In With Mandatory Fun "Part 1""

Video.summary: CA revisits some of the best moments of the year and some never before seen footage!
Video.title: "Bonus Round"

Video.summary: In part 1 of the British episodes, Andy starts his road trip around the United Kingdom in the Cotswolds where he sets out to hunt and taste the beautiful venison that Michelin star chef, Mark Robinson has to offer. He then proceeds to travel to the border of Scotland where he samples some of the finest and most historic bird shooting practices found throughout the British Isles.
Video.title: Wildbound - "UK Part 1"

Video.summary: Heading to one of the great wilderness areas of North America, Dan Cabela is in search of Stone Sheep in British Columbia. Joining scientists, they embark on an ambitious conservation project to help conserve this incredible species.
Video.title: "Keeping Sheep on the Mountains"

Video.summary: Join Dan Cabela as he travels across Argentina to see how scientists and hunters are working together. From region-wide bird surveys to the incredible conservation work being done on the critically endangered huemul deer, this beautiful country has no shortage of fascinating stories.
Video.title: "Conservation in La Albiceleste"

Video.summary: Over a decade after the game changing Longbeard XR was introduced by Winchester, the wait is finally over for their next ground breaking turkey load. After years of perfecting the recipe behind the scenes, Winchester finally unveiled the new Longbeard TSS at a turkey camp in northern Michigan. First watch the jaw dropping Longbeard TSS pattern board testing, then see it go to work on some spring gobblers.
Video.title: "Longbeard Mitten Mayhem"

Video.summary: It’s been 60 years since AA was introduced to the shooting sports world. Since that day, an immeasurable number of AA hulls have hit the ground and countless shooters have stood center stage raising a trophy where millions of dollars have been won. Professional and recreational shooters alike have trusted AA for six decades. In this episode, load up for stories of high school, collegiate competitors and the world’s best as they bust clays with the legendary shot shell. - GUESTS: Zach Keenbaum (Team Winchester) Desi Edmunds (Team Winchester) Kayla Wilgus (Team Winchester) Amy Cawley (Team Winchester) Anthony Matarese (Team Winchester)
Video.title: "Six Decades Of Dominance"

Video.summary: Everything is bigger in Texas, including the stories. With the whitetail rut in the midwest winding down, the Winchester Life Crew of Chris Keefer, Casey Keefer, Nikki Boxler and Paul Sawyer head south to the Texas Panhandle with Winchesters in hand hoping to wrangle in a few Texas-sized giants.
Video.title: “Lonestar Legends”

Video.summary: The positive impact made by the shooting sports and recreational shooting to people across our country can’t be measured in just one story, so we have three. In Florida, Olympian Dania Vizzi trains at her home shooting facility and talk about how she went from ballerina to one of the best shotgun shooters in the world. In Nebraska, firearms instructor Thea Van Syoc has used the disciplines of competitive pistol shooting and training to help shape her life. Finally we’ll take a look into the National African American Gun Association and learn how one day at the range for Phillip Smith created the fastest growing firearm organization in the United States.
Video.title: “Shooting is Life”

Video.summary: Nikki Boxler’s life in Update New York on her family farm raising cattle and producing maple syrup is a few thousand miles from the hills of New Mexico. We’ll take a look into Nikki’s life back home and then follow along as she visits the small town of Quemado and its bustling population of 228, while she pursues bugling elk with her favorite Winchester in hand. We’ll also head to Missouri and Texas to chase whitetails with Chris Keefer and Paul Sawyer.
Video.title: “New Mexico Magic”

Video.summary: We set out to tell the tale of Marion - and his legendary Mack’s Prairie Wings, smack dab in the middle of the world famous Stuttgart, Arkansas - the land of ducks and rice. Get an inside look at a man that many consider to be larger than life, from those that knew him best, like his cousin and fellow hell raiser, Bud McCollum. We’ll even hear a story or two from Milton Stovesand, an 81 year old former waterfowl guide and Mack’s employee who actually guided John Olin a time or two, and ran the timber holes with Marion. It’s timber holes and tall tales. It’s cups of coffee and cupped up wings. It’s partners in business, and partners in potholes and it’s pure Arkansas...just like Marion.
Video.title: “The Legend of Stuttgart”

Video.summary: In this episode, we catch up with and old friend of ours, Carl Parks. Carl's son CJ was killed in a tragic accident 6 years ago and we're gonna spend a day of fishing and reflection and celebration of CJ's life; We spent time with Carl years ago not long after the accident and filmed and episode called "The Wake" and now want to catch up again to showcase Carl's faith and strength that's helped him with his journey in life after losing his son.
Video.title: "The Wake Continues"

Video.summary: In this episode, Mark is joined by Matt Ogle, a proffesional bowler that Mark has known since Matt was 10 years old. Mark has been close to their family and has Matt grow into the bowling pro he is today. Matt was raised to love what he does…bassin and bowling the same way his Dad loved doing both. Matt's Dad actually ran the NABA Tournament Trail in KY for many years; Matt won the 2019 PBA Doubles Championship and the 2023 PBA Championship.
Video.title: "Bassin' & Bowling"

Video.summary: Capt. Jared makes his way to Bolivar, Texas with Clay Guest & Ryan Pyburn.
Video.title: "Texas Mo Joe"

Video.summary: Capt. Jared Raskob joins legendary Captains Donny Lange and Charlie Scoble offshore in Islamorada.
Video.title: "Tackle Center"

Video.summary: Capt. Jared Raskob joins his long time friend Capt. Jake Roth for a day of fishing in the Florida Keys.
Video.title: "Jake Roth"

Video.summary: Mitch Widom, Andy Yaffa, & Chris Hammaway join Capt. Jared Raskob in Los Cabos on the hunt for Striped Marlin Part 2.
Video.title: "Cabo Marlin Part 2"

Video.summary: In this episode of Local Lines Brandon Branch fishes Ozello Florida.
Video.title: "Day Saver"

Video.summary: In this episode of Local Lines Television, Brandon is fishing with Steve Grant of Diamond Fishing Products.
Video.title: "Wahoo Shootout"

Video.summary: In Today's episode, Brandon is going to be fishing with Larry from Save Crystal River. They will be targeting Redfish, Trout, and Bass.
Video.title: "Kings Bay"

Video.summary: Today Capt. Brandon is fishing the big bend with Capt. Matt.
Video.title: "Horseshoe Beach"

Video.summary: Today, Capt. Danny is in Stuart, FL. Joined by 16-year-old angler, Denton Potter. The two are targeting the local bridge dwelling giants, while going “Beyond The Surface” into Denton’s story.
Video.title: "Young Gun"

Video.summary: In this episode Slade travels to Kansas to try and bag one of the Midwest's most impressive deer!
Video.title: "150 Inch + Kansas Giant"
